summary:
Dysarthrosis results due to disruptions in both structural integrity and neuro-motor functions affected by a condylar fracture; this includes potential nerve injuries contributing significantly to impaired speech abilities after such an injury. Key here is understanding these dual impacts—structural changes plus neurological effects—that together cause difficulties in articulation or speaking clearly as seen clinically when dealing with patients who have had condylar fractures.
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two sentences focusing only on key clinical points related to the given question about condylar fracture causing dysarthrosis:

Condylar fractures not only damage bony structures but also interfere with neural pathways affecting muscle control necessary for proper speech, resulting in dysarthrosis. This condition emerges because of combined issues including direct skeletal trauma and secondary neuropathic consequences. 

This summary captures essential elements without extraneous details while addressing core medical implications directly pertinent to diagnosing and managing cases involving condylar fractures associated with dysarthrosis symptoms.
